results.possibleIdentity: Gerald Present is a physicist and scientific journalist who was active in the New York City scientific and technical community from the late 1950s through at least the 1980s. He earned a B.A. from Columbia College (Class of 1957) and subsequently received his Ph.D. in Physics. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he held positions as a scientific journalist and editor, including an affiliation with the Polytechnic Institute of Brooklyn. He was a prominent figure at the American Institute of Physics (AIP), where he served as an associate editor for 'Physics Today' and as a staff scientist in the physics-information division before being appointed senior science writer for the AIP public information division in 1973. In this capacity, he edited publications such as 'Physics News in 1977' and managed press relations for major technical conferences, including the 1975 Conference on Laser Engineering and Applications (CLEA). In the late 1980s, Present served as a science writer and spokesperson for the IBM Thomas J. Watson Research Center, where he communicated high-profile breakthroughs in fields such as high-temperature superconductivity and pen-based computing. He is also associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, likely in an editorial or communication capacity related to their newsletters and technical publications.
